subject: Product Management
title: Steve Jobs: The Anti-PM PM
description: A quick, practical guide to Steve Jobs: The Anti-PM PM.

hook.text: Steve Jobs never held the title 'product manager.' But he's the most famous product thinker in history.

buildup.text: Jobs obsessed over every detail — the weight of the iPhone, the curve of a corner, the unboxing experience.

discovery.text: He didn't use focus groups or A/B tests. He believed customers couldn't tell you what they wanted next.

twist.text: His approach broke every PM rule: no user research, no data-driven decisions, pure product intuition.

climax.text: Jobs proved that taste and vision can substitute for process — but only if you're right almost every time.

punchline.text: He broke every rule. And it worked. Mostly.

quiz.question: What was unusual about Steve Jobs' approach to product management?
options: He relied on intuition and taste rather than user research and data
He followed standard PM processes rigorously
He delegated all product decisions to engineers
